# The Cedar Case

By dusk, Boxwell reached the Brambled Inn, rain dripping from the brim of his hat.
Everyone in three counties knew Boxwell as a clockmaker, and he never travelled
without the cedar case. The Brambled Inn had kept the same stopped clock for
twenty years, and Boxwell laid his cedar case beside the stopped hallway clock
before he had even asked for a room.

That night, turning brass gears under lamplight, he noticed the escapement
spring was not broken. It was missing — removed, cleanly, by someone who knew
exactly what they were doing.
